trigger_keywords: nuez caramelo dulzor elevado y alta acidez

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Fortified Madeira is exposed to heat and oxygen during maturation, either more rapidly by estufagem or more gradually by canteiro.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Heat and controlled oxidation accelerate reactions that transform fresh fruit aromas while the wine's naturally high acidity remains structurally important.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Mature Madeira can develop nut, caramel and dried-fruit complexity while retaining balancing acidity; Malmsey also retains pronounced sweetness.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: heat and oxidative maturation explain Madeira's developed nutty and caramel character, while acidity balances sweeter styles.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
